assumptions: The question is about hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS for the foreign exchange risk arising from an intragroup dividend-related balance or transaction.
The objective is to identify the relevant top-level IFRS hedge accounting models, not to assess eligibility or documentation details.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats a recognised asset or liability, including qualifying foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements, within the recognised-item hedge family mapped to the fair value hedge model.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately treats highly probable forecast transactions, including qualifying foreign currency risk on certain intragroup forecast transactions in consolidated financial statements, within the forecast-transaction hedge family mapped to the cash flow hedge model.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedging of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model relevant to foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.
